title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Iran Denies Any Agreement On Nuclear Site Inspections
stories.summary:
Iran Denies Any Agreement On Nuclear Site Inspections. Iran’s Foreign Ministry flatly contradicted U.S. claims that Tehran had agreed to allow international nuclear inspectors into the country, setting up a sharp public dispute over the outcome of talks in Switzerland.

Spokesman Esmail Baghaei said Iran had held no meeting with International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) Director General Rafael Grossi and had made no ... Al Jazeera: Trump disputes Iran’s rejection of nuclear inspectors.

Al Jazeera reports that president Trump pushed back on Iran’s foreign ministry claim that there are no plans to allow IAEA nuclear inspectors.

stories.title: Antifa Terror Cell Leader In Anti-ICE Ambush Gets Hefty Prison Sentence
stories.summary:
Antifa Terror Cell Leader In Anti-ICE Ambush Gets Hefty Prison Sentence. An Antifa anarchist who led an ambush at a Texas Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) detention center last year will spend a century behind bars.

Prosecutors claimed former Marine Corps Reservist Benjamin Hanil Song shot an officer during the July 4, 2025, attack before running and hiding from authorities for a week. On Tuesday, he ...

BBC: Eight sentenced to 450 years in prison over anti-ICE riot where officer was shot. BBC reports that an officer was shot in the neck during the Texas disorder by "Antifa Cell operatives", prosecutors said.

stories.title: Savannah Guthrie Begs for Information After Ransom Note Claims Mom is Dead: 'Somebody Knows Something'
stories.summary:
Savannah Guthrie Begs for Information After Ransom Note Claims Mom is Dead: 'Somebody Knows Something'. NBC correspondent Savannah Guthrie is begging for information after a ransom note claimed her mother is dead.

"Somebody knows something," the Today show host said through tears.

The post Savannah Guthrie Begs for Information After Ransom Note Claims Mom is Dead: ‘Somebody Knows Something’ appeared first on <. BBC: 'We're begging' - Savannah Guthrie pleads for help as details of her mother’s case emerge.

stories.title: US top court says Rastafarian man cannot sue prison guards who cut his dreadlocks
stories.summary:

Al Jazeera: US Supreme Court says Rastafarian man shaved by prison guards can’t sue.

Al Jazeera reports that the high court has upheld ruling that prisoners cannot sue prison staff for money damages under religious liberties law.

stories.title: ‘Navigating the unknown together’: me and my idiot AI boyfriend
stories.summary:
‘Navigating the unknown together’: me and my idiot AI boyfriend. I believe that chatbots have no place in a decent society, and am repelled by the topic of AI in general.

But could I be seduced?

I received a text message from my editor: “Um, is it unethical to ask you to get an AI bf?? You can prob say no.” Resentment.

Contempt! Sorrow.

Unease. I love text messaging.

I have text message exchanges with, let’s say, 15 people a day. If you want me to do something, you should ask via text message.

My editor knows this. She also knows, though it’s m.

stories.title: Calamity state to stay in Negros Occidental amid infestation
stories.summary:
Calamity state to stay in Negros Occidental amid infestation. BACOLOD CITY, NEGROS OCCIDENTAL, Philippines — The state of calamity in Negros Occidental will remain in effect as Red Striped Soft-Scale Insects (RSSI) have infested 32.18 percent of the province’s sugarcane fields, Gov. Eugenio Jose Lacson said Tuesday.

Sugar Regulatory Administration (SRA) chief Pablo Luis Azcona and United Sugar Producers Federation president Manuel Lamata briefed.

stories.title: UN rapporteur should also talk to ex-rebels, says NTF-Elcac
stories.summary:
UN rapporteur should also talk to ex-rebels, says NTF-Elcac.

MANILA, Philippines — The National Task Force to End Local Communist Conflict (NTF-Elcac) has urged a United Nations special rapporteur to hear the testimonies of former rebels after she flagged the use of counterterrorism laws to prosecute journalists and human rights advocates.

stories.title: PDEA agents nab farmer in Sulu drug bust
stories.summary:
PDEA agents nab farmer in Sulu drug bust. PAGADIAN CITY, ZAMBOANGA DEL SUR, Philippines — A joint anti-drug operation led by the Philippine Drug Enforcement Agency (PDEA)–Sulu Provincial Office, backed by various law enforcement, military, and intelligence units, arrested a farmer for selling meth to an undercover agent in Barangay Lianutan of Luuk, Sulu on Sunday morning.

stories.title: Germany's Economic Model Is Broken, and No One Has a Plan B
stories.summary:
Germany's Economic Model Is Broken, and No One Has a Plan B. The country is focused on exports, but China is slowing imports and U.S. tariff threats are growing.

Politicians are offering few alternatives.

stories.title: Met to expand use of live facial recognition into central London by Christmas
stories.summary:
Met to expand use of live facial recognition into central London by Christmas. Technology to be used in six more areas next year as critics say tens of thousands of people will be forced into ‘digital police lineup’ The Metropolitan police is to expand its use of live facial recognition (LFR) technology, first into London’s West End by Christmas and then into a further six areas next year.

The new cameras will be fixed, and could be attached to street furniture such as lamp-posts. Critics said the new plans mean tens of thousands of people will be forced int.
